distinctive_features: hollow structure enclosing a plume of descending brine
resembles an inverted chimney of ice
thin, fragile walls that thicken over time
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: can reach from the underside of sea ice to the seafloor, depending on conditions
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: vertical, extending downward from sea ice

domain_specific_visual.formation_environment: beneath developing sea ice in polar oceans
domain_specific_visual.growth_pattern: grows downward as brine flows and freezes

functional_attributes.primary_function: formation of ice around descending brine
secondary_functions: can trap sea creatures in ice

domain_specific_functional.formation_conditions: requires supercooled, saline water and minimal currents

relationships.description: Brinicles form beneath developing sea ice as brine is expelled.
The descending brine interacts with surrounding seawater to form ice.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: documented in scientific research and media
